tools.name: list_posts
tools.description: Return up to N most recent published blog posts.

tools.name: search_posts
tools.description: Case-insensitive substring match across title, description, and tags. Returns up to 10 matches.

tools.name: search_site
tools.description: Semantic (vector similarity) search across blog posts and projects — the same Cloudflare Vectorize retrieval the Ask chatbot uses, without the LLM call. Broader than search_posts (which only does exact substring matching on title/description/tags): finds conceptually related content even when the qu

tools.name: get_post
tools.description: Return the full markdown body and frontmatter of a post by slug.

tools.name: list_projects
tools.description: Return all portfolio projects with their slug, title, and short description.

tools.name: get_project
tools.description: Return the full markdown body and frontmatter of a project by slug.

tools.name: get_about
tools.description: Return a short bio about Cameron Rye, the portfolio owner.

tools.name: get_now
tools.description: Return what Cameron is currently working on, learning, building, reading, and up to in life — the live /now page sections.

tools.name: get_reading
tools.description: Return Cameron's reading list — books currently reading, finished, or abandoned, with notes. Filter by status if given.

tools.name: get_pulse
tools.description: Return live, PII-free operational stats for rye.dev: recent Core Web Vitals p75s, 30-day pageviews, trailing-30d uptime %, top-reacted pages, and the last deploy. Same data the homepage pulse strip shows.

tools.name: submit_contact
tools.description: Send Cameron a message through the site contact form. Subject to the same validation and per-IP rate limits as the contact form itself.

tools.name: subscribe_newsletter
tools.description: Subscribe an email address to the newsletter. Sends a confirmation email; the subscription only becomes active once confirmed. Same validation and per-IP rate limits as the signup form.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: list_posts, search_posts, search_site, get_post · Private data: submit_contact · Public sink: get_post, submit_contact, subscribe_newsletter.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: list_posts, search_posts, search_site, get_post · Private data: submit_contact · Public sink: get_post, submit_contact, subscribe_newsletter

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
